title: assembly
description:
Articulated-asset intermediate representation. Consumed by blender_assemble.py (visual render) and build_urdf.py (URDF export).

TRANSFORM CHAIN (applied by blender_assemble.py):
  world_pos = parent_scale × (origin_xyz + child_scale × raw_mesh_center)

SCALE INHERITANCE RULE (non-optional):
  child_scale[axis] = target_world_size[axis] / (parent_scale[axis] × raw_GLB_size[axis])
  Using child_scale=[1,1,1] when parent_scale≠[1,1,1] produces a wrongly-sized mesh.
  Use compute_placement_scales.py to pre-compute correct values.

BACK-COMPUTING origin_xyz FROM TARGET WORLD POSITION:
  origin_xyz[axis] = target_world_center[axis] / parent_scale[axis]
                     − child_scale[axis] × raw_mesh_center[axis]
  When rpy_deg is non-zero, first rotate (child_scale × raw_mesh_center) by R_Z(rpy_deg).

root.description: Base directory for resolving relative mesh paths (the run directory).

robot_name.description: Name of the assembled object; used as the URDF <robot name>.

parent.description: Name of the parent link; null for the root. The child's origin.xyz and scale are expressed in the parent's local (unscaled) coordinate space. The parent's scale multiplies the child's world position.

visual_mesh.description: GLB path (relative to root or absolute) used for high-quality Blender renders.

collision_mesh.description: Simplified OBJ path (relative to root or absolute) used for URDF visual/collision geometry.

origin.description: Pose of this link's pivot in the parent's local (unscaled) coordinate space. For the root link this is a world-space pose.

xyz.description: Pivot position [x, y, z] in parent-local space. To place the mesh center at world position T: origin_xyz = T / parent_scale − child_scale × raw_mesh_center.

rpy_deg.description: Pivot rotation in degrees, XYZ Euler (roll, pitch, yaw). Rotation is applied BEFORE scale when computing the mesh contribution. For revolute doors: rpy_deg[2]<0 opens a left-hinged door forward (−Y); rpy_deg[2]>0 opens a right-hinged door forward.

scale.description: Mesh scale [sx, sy, sz] applied in the Blender assembly. MUST account for parent scale inheritance: child_scale[axis] = target_world_size[axis] / (parent_scale[axis] × raw_GLB_size[axis]). Non-uniform values are expected when the GLB proportions differ from target proportions.
